tree.name: Ziaire Williams
tree.tradeDescription: Nets traded Mamadi Diakite and draft rights to Nemanja Dangubic to Grizzlies for Ziaire Williams and a 2030 2nd (DAL own). Brooklyn also received a trade exception. Memphis also received a trade exception.
tree.note: Stanford wing, #10 pick in 2021 draft by Memphis. Young upside play for BKN's rebuild.

assetsGivenUp.name: Mamadi Diakite
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Acquired from NYK as part of the Mikal Bridges trade. Knicks sent Bogdanovic, Diakite, Shake Milton, 5 first-round picks, a 2nd-round pick, and a swap to Nets for Bridges, Keita Bates-Diop, Vaulet, and a 2026 2nd.
assetsGivenUp.note: Virginia big man. Came to BKN in the massive Mikal Bridges trade haul, immediately flipped to Memphis for Ziaire Williams.

assetsGivenUp.name: Mikal Bridges
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Acquired from Phoenix in Kevin Durant trade. BKN sent KD and TJ Warren to PHX for Cam Johnson, Mikal Bridges, 4 first-round picks, and pick swap rights.
assetsGivenUp.note: Villanova product, elite 3-and-D wing. Acquired in KD trade, became centerpiece BKN flipped to NYK for massive pick haul.

assetsGivenUp.name: Kevin Durant
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Sign-and-trade: Nets traded D'Angelo Russell, Treveon Graham, and Shabazz Napier to Warriors for Kevin Durant. Conditional 2020 1st did not convey; became 2025 2nd (Javon Small).
assetsGivenUp.note: 4x scoring champ, 2x Finals MVP. Signed with BKN as FA, structured as sign-and-trade with GSW.

assetsGivenUp.name: D'Angelo Russell
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Nets traded Brook Lopez and the #27 pick (Kyle Kuzma) to Lakers for D'Angelo Russell and Timofey Mozgov.
assetsGivenUp.note: Young guard, traded from LAL to BKN alongside Mozgov's contract. Became All-Star in Brooklyn before KD sign-and-trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Brook Lopez
assetsGivenUp.note: Drafted #10 overall by New Jersey Nets in 2008. Franchise cornerstone for a decade. Sent to LAL in the D'Angelo Russell trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2017 1st Round Pick (#27 via pick swap)
assetsGivenUp.note: BKN had the worst record in 2017 (#1 pick), but owed it to BOS from the KG/Pierce trade. BOS swapped #1 to PHI for #3 (Jayson Tatum). BKN got BOS's pick position (#27) via the swap. LAL drafted Kyle Kuzma.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2017 1st Round Pick (#1 overall)
assetsGivenUp.note: BKN's own pick — worst record in NBA, #1 overall. Owed to BOS from the 2013 KG/Pierce trade. BOS traded #1 to PHI for #3 (Jayson Tatum) and a future 1st. PHI drafted Markelle Fultz.

assetsGivenUp.name: Treveon Graham
assetsGivenUp.note: Signed as free agent by BKN. Sent to GSW in KD sign-and-trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Shabazz Napier
assetsGivenUp.note: UConn guard, signed as free agent by BKN (2yr deal, July 2018). Sent to GSW in KD sign-and-trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: TJ Warren
assetsGivenUp.note: Signed as free agent by BKN. Sent to PHX in Kevin Durant trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Nemanja Dangubic
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: BKN traded DeMarre Carroll to SAS; SAS traded Nemanja Dangubic to BKN and Davis Bertans to WAS; WAS traded Aaron White to BKN.
assetsGivenUp.note: Serbian guard, stash player. Draft rights acquired by BKN in the DeMarre Carroll 3-team trade. Sent to Memphis in Ziaire Williams trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: DeMarre Carroll
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Raptors traded DeMarre Carroll, a 2018 1st-round pick, and a 2018 2nd-round pick to BKN for Justin Hamilton. Salary dump for Toronto.
assetsGivenUp.note: Veteran wing, acquired from Toronto as a salary dump (BKN got picks too). Sent to SAS in 3-team deal that brought Dangubic.

assetsGivenUp.name: Justin Hamilton
assetsGivenUp.note: Center, signed as free agent by BKN (2yr/$6M, July 2016). Sent to TOR in DeMarre Carroll salary dump trade.
